rationale: Both papers involve goal-dependent consumer states (the cited paper’s browsing vs. buying goals and the citing paper’s informational vs. transactional stages), so the citation could plausibly serve as conceptual support. But the substantive domains differ (price image/line extensions vs. online search/construal matching), and the matched evidence is only a DOI entry with no in-text citation context, so there is not enough to confidently keep or drop.
